titel: The Commission is simplifying and making energy labeling and tire labeling more accessible for European consumers.
html: Press release from the European Commission, Brussels, June 24, 2026. European consumers, suppliers, and retailers of household appliances, electronic products, and tires will benefit from simplified labeling regulations proposed by the European Commission today.
tldr: The EU wants to make it easier for everyone to understand the labeling on tires and appliances. This is intended to help people make better choices when they are shopping.
lattlast:
The European Commission, which is the governing body of the EU, has proposed new regulations. The proposal concerns the design of labels on various products.

This applies to products commonly used in everyday life, such as tires and household appliances like washing machines. It also applies to electronic products.

The goal is to make the labeling more easily readable and understandable for everyone living in Europe. It should be easier for both consumers and retailers to know what the products contain.

The new regulations are intended to make it easier for consumers to compare different products when shopping. This applies to anyone who buys or sells these goods within the EU.
